module1.clean_text:
Hospitalization report 

               The child [PATIENT] aged [AGE] years and [AGE] originally from and residing in Oran, followed in our department since the age of [AGE] for post-measles COPD and primary immunodeficiency type "Agammaglobulinemia" with severe neutropenia admitted to our level for treatment of immunoglobulin N 75 and for exploration and management of vomiting and chronic abdominal pain. History:
Family:
-Father: [AGE] years old, O positive, smoker
-Mother: [AGE] years old, A positive, dermatological lesions not documented
-Siblings: 1-Boy [AGE] years old, eczema, screening done (negative). Personal:
Physiological:
Poorly monitored pregnancy, delivery 36 weeks by upper route indicated: for closed cervix
Neonatal resuscitation: present. Apgar not mentioned in the health record. Birth weight: 03 kg 300
Exclusive maternal breastfeeding
Anti-rachitis prophylaxis received at the age of 1 [AGE] and 6 [AGE] vaccination delay
Psychomotor development: judged good
Academic performance: average
Personal atopy: no
Pathological:
Neonatal period:
-Neonatal jaundice put on phototherapy. - Hospitalized for 07 days in the neonatal period for early respiratory distress and late prematurity of 36 [AGE] having benefited from antibiotic therapy. -At the age of 1 and a half [AGE] Severe bronchiolitis
- Age of [AGE] and a half [AGE] Measles complicated by pneumonia. History of illness:
Main diagnosis: DIP agammaglobulinemia type. Associated diagnoses: Post-measles COPD. Date of diagnosis: [DATE] Age of diagnosis: [AGE] Diagnostic elements:
Clinic:
Bronchial congestion from birth
Measles at the age of [AGE] complicated by measles pneumonia. Organic:
Immune assessment [DATE] EHU
IgA<0.2 g/L 6 to 12 [AGE] (0.13-0.82)
IgG1 1.65 g/L 1-1 and ½ (2.5-8.2)
IgG2 < 0.2g/L 1-1 and ½ (0.38-2.4)
IgG 4 < 0.03 g/L 1-1 and ½ (<0.03-0.62)
Immune assessment at Béni Messous hospital (06/10/20)
IgG=12.07g/l IgA=less than 0.02g/l IgM=0.19g/L
Absence of LB with profound reduction of NK. Normal intracytoplasmic expression of the Btk gene in monocytes in the patient and his mother compared to a control subject. The diagnosis in favor of congenital Agammaglobulinemia associated with severe neutropenia, The association of severe neutropenia with congenital agammaglobulinemia linked to X "Bruton's disease" is often described in the literature. Radiological:
Front chest x-ray: (07/14/2019): left pericardial opacity with bilateral infiltrate
Cervico-thoracic CT (01/15/2019) clinical “Benserai”: objectifying:
Discreet thickening of the ary-epiglottic folds more marked on the right without associated cervical anomaly, Reticular infiltrate with right hilo-apical alveolar condensation
Processing in progress:
Itraconazole: 5 mg/kg/day
Bactrim 400mg/80mg/day
Twice-daily physiotherapy
Budecort 200ug 2 */D
Repercussions of the illness and clarification:
Nutritionally:
Failure to thrive: Weight: 17kg (-2.2DS) Height: 123 cm (+0.1DS). Moderate to severe malnutrition: Gomez: 72% Waterloo: 75% Moderate underweight
Nutritional assessment: 24/23/2026: protein-energy malnutrition
On the pulmonary level:
No signs of chronic respiratory failure. ECBC: 02/28/2026: No germ
EFR 16/12/24: within the limits of the standard. Gait test: without abnormality, no desaturation or dyspnea on exertion after 6 minutes. Thoracic CT 02/24/2026: Unremarkable examination
On the infectious level:
*Skin: no cutaneous or mucosal mycoses and no skin infections. *Digestive: Chronic abdominal pain with vomiting
Abdominal CT 02/26/2026: Cecal ileocolitis with an inflammatory/infectious appearance
On the cardiac level:
B1B2 audible, no added noise or hiss, HR: 88 b/min
Absence of signs of chronic cor pulmonale
Cardiac Doppler ultrasound 11/21/2024: Within normal limits. Intermediate history: since the last visit 01/24/2026
Several consultations at the Canastel UMC level for abdominal pain and vomiting benefiting from symptomatic treatment
HISTORY OF ILLNESS in relation to the reason for hospitalization: 02/23/2026
Goes back 3 months previously with the gradual onset of abdominal pain;
vomiting motivating the mother consulted HDJ pulmonology or an abdominopelvic ultrasound done returning without anomaly supplemented by a FOGD + biopsy showing HP gastritis. A gastropediatric opinion was requested, the child was put on triple therapy but given the lack of clinical improvement and the appearance of weight loss with deterioration in general condition, the child was hospitalized for further exploration and treatment.
Clinical examination at admission: 02/23/2026
Anthropometric measurements:
Weight: 17kg (-2.2 DS) Height: 123 cm (+0.1 DS) BMI: 11.2kg (-2.9 DS) Severe weight loss
P/A: 72% Moderate underweight P/T: 75% Moderate thinness
- Average general condition, afebrile, integuments and conjunctiva pals
Pleuropulmonary examination: eupneic, FR: 20 cycles/min, No signs of respiratory distress, SaO2: 98%, Clear auscultation in both lung fields
Cardiovascular examination: HR: 122 b/min, CRT < 3 sec, Auscultation: B1, B2 audible properties, absence of added noises
Abdominopelvic examination: Soft abdomen, no organomegaly, free hernia orifices, preserved transit, early vomiting of food
ENT: clean throat
Review of other devices: Is unremarkable
What to do:
-Hospitalization
-Condition, peripheral venous route
- Immunoglobulin infusion at a rate of 0.8g/kg to be administered over 4 hours
-Double antibiotic therapy: claforan and flagyl
-Dietician advice: put the child on oliclinomel
-Complete biological assessment
-Plan an abdominopelvic ultrasound or abdominal CT
Admission assessment: 02/23/2026
SNF:
GB: 1870 elements PNN: 770Lymph760 i.e. leuko-neutro-lymphopenia
Hb: 11.5g/dl VGM: 98.2fl CCMH: 32.2 g/dl
Platelet: 238,000 elements TP: 90.2% TCK: 37 SecFibrinogene: 3.2 g/l
FSP: Leukocytes: Leukopenia 1870, PNN: 780 G/L Lympho: 820 G/L
Platelets: Very rich in platelets, predominance of microthrombocytes, presence of a few large or even giant platelets, presence of small platelet aggregates
Erythrocytes: Anisocytosis with a macrocytic tendency, presence of a few schistocytes,
Presence of 4% of suspicious cells
Blood ionogram: Na 130 K: 3.84 Cl: 95
Urea: 0.14g/lCreat: 3.98mg/l
AST: 24.5 ALT: 9.5
gGT: 10.32 TG: 1.06 g/l Cholesterol: 0.92
Albumin: 30g/l Uric acid: 9.29
Abdominopelvic ultrasound: without abnormality
Thoraco-abdomino-pelvic CT: cecal ileocolitis with an inflammatory or infectious appearance
Stool coproparasitology: negative
Quantify: positive
BK PCR: negative
Gastric tubing looking for BK: negative direct examination
Autoimmunity assessment: in progress
CAT: given the results of the abdominopelvic CT and will quantify it as positive:
- Patient presented to the gastropediatrics department in Canastel for a possible colonoscopy + biopsy, but patient refused given the absence of a probe adapted to his age and weight
- anti-tuberculosis treatment was started on day 9 of hospitalization based on 2RHZE/4RH
Evolution:
During his hospitalization
Clinically:
-a weight gain of 1KG
-disappearance of vomiting but persistence of abdominal pain
On the paraclinical level:
FNS with FS: GB: 2266 PNN: 540 LYM: 1620 i.e. leuko-neutropenia
FSP: Significant anisopoikilocytosis, presence of macrocytes, numerous schistocytes, a few target cells
Plq: rich with numerous platelet aggregates
A hematology opinion made: indication of PMO but patient refused by the intensive care team
The patient is discharged on 03/19/2026 with a check-up appointment on 03/23/2026 with letter for colonoscopy with biopsy. To do and collect:
-Recovers the autoimmunity assessment
-Retrieves the results of the immunodeficiency assessment of the patient and the mother
- Retrieve gastric tube results
-Do the colonoscopy + biopsy
-Represent the patient to hematology for a marrow puncture.

ordo_candidates.name: MHC class II deficiency
ordo_candidates.name_fr: Déficit en molécules HLA de classe II

phenopacket.input:
Hospitalization report 

               The child SAIDANI Mohamed Seif Eddine, aged 7 years and 5 months; originally from and residing in Oran, followed in our department since the age of 11 months for post-measles COPD and primary immunodeficiency type "Agammaglobulinemia" with severe neutropenia admitted to our level for treatment of immunoglobulin N 75 and for exploration and management of vomiting and chronic abdominal pain. History:
Family:
-Father: 32 years old, O positive, smoker
-Mother: 32 years old, A positive, dermatological lesions not documented
-Siblings: 1-Boy 11 years old, eczema, screening done (negative). Personal:
Physiological:
Poorly monitored pregnancy, delivery 36 weeks by upper route indicated: for closed cervix
Neonatal resuscitation: present. Apgar not mentioned in the health record. Birth weight: 03 kg 300
Exclusive maternal breastfeeding
Anti-rachitis prophylaxis received at the age of 1 month and 6 months
Vaccination: vaccination delay
Psychomotor development: judged good
Academic performance: average
Personal atopy: no
Pathological:
Neonatal period:
-Neonatal jaundice put on phototherapy. - Hospitalized for 07 days in the neonatal period for early respiratory distress and late prematurity of 36 weeks having benefited from antibiotic therapy. -At the age of 1 and a half months: Severe bronchiolitis
- Age of 02 and a half months: Measles complicated by pneumonia. History of illness:
Main diagnosis: DIP agammaglobulinemia type. Associated diagnoses: Post-measles COPD. Date of diagnosis: 09/16/2019. Age of diagnosis: 11 months. Diagnostic elements:

Bronchial congestion from birth
Measles at the age of 2 months complicated by measles pneumonia. Organic:

IgA<0.2 g/L 6 to 12 months (0.13-0.82)
IgG1 1.65 g/L 1-1 and ½ (2.5-8.2)

Immune assessment at Béni Messous hospital (06/10/20)
IgG=12.07g/l IgA=less than 0.02g/l IgM=0.19g/L
Absence of LB with profound reduction of NK. Normal intracytoplasmic expression of the Btk gene in monocytes in the patient and his mother compared to a control subject. The diagnosis in favor of congenital Agammaglobulinemia associated with severe neutropenia, The association of severe neutropenia with congenital agammaglobulinemia linked to X "Bruton's disease" is often described in the literature. Front chest x-ray: (07/14/2019): left pericardial opacity with bilateral infiltrate

Discreet thickening of the ary-epiglottic folds more marked on the right without associated cervical anomaly, Reticular infiltrate with right hilo-apical alveolar condensation
Processing in progress:
Itraconazole: 5 mg/kg/day
Bactrim 400mg/80mg/day
Twice-daily physiotherapy
Budecort 200ug 2 */D

Nutritionally:

Nutritional assessment: 24/23/2026: protein-energy malnutrition

No signs of chronic respiratory failure. ECBC: 02/28/2026: No germ

*Skin: no cutaneous or mucosal mycoses and no skin infections. *Digestive: Chronic abdominal pain with vomiting
Abdominal CT 02/26/2026: Cecal ileocolitis with an inflammatory/infectious appearance
On the cardiac level:
B1B2 audible, no added noise or hiss, HR: 88 b/min
Absence of signs of chronic cor pulmonale
Cardiac Doppler ultrasound 11/21/2024: Within normal limits. Intermediate history: since the last visit 01/24/2026
Several consultations at the Canastel UMC level for abdominal pain and vomiting benefiting from symptomatic treatment
HISTORY OF ILLNESS in relation to the reason for hospitalization: 02/23/2026
Goes back 3 months previously with the gradual onset of abdominal pain;
vomiting motivating the mother consulted HDJ pulmonology or an abdominopelvic ultrasound done returning without anomaly supplemented by a FOGD + biopsy showing HP gastritis. A gastropediatric opinion was requested, the child was put on triple therapy but given the lack of clinical improvement and the appearance of weight loss with deterioration in general condition, the child was hospitalized for further exploration and treatment.
Clinical examination at admission: 02/23/2026
Anthropometric measurements:
Weight: 17kg (-2.2 DS) Height: 123 cm (+0.1 DS) BMI: 11.2kg (-2.9 DS) Severe weight loss
P/A: 72% Moderate underweight P/T: 75% Moderate thinness
- Average general condition, afebrile, integuments and conjunctiva pals
Pleuropulmonary examination: eupneic, FR: 20 cycles/min, No signs of respiratory distress, SaO2: 98%, Clear auscultation in both lung fields

Abdominopelvic examination: Soft abdomen, no organomegaly, free hernia orifices, preserved transit, early vomiting of food
